name: I have carpal tunnel from repetitive factory work. Is that considered an occupational illness under Utah law?

mainEntity.name: I have carpal tunnel from repetitive factory work. Is that considered an occupational illness under Utah law?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, repetitive motion carpal tunnel is covered if job-related with medical proof.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, carpal tunnel from repetitive motion qualifies as occupational disease under Utah’s AOE/COE test.[1] Workers must show medical causation, but broad coverage applies.[1] Key factors: (1) Employment creates risk; (2) Occurs advancing employer’s business; (3) Hearing loss or dermatitis also covered.[1] Seek doctor confirmation linking to job, report to employer, file claim for medical/wage benefits.[1] Don’t delay reporting.

description: Yes, repetitive motion carpal tunnel is covered if job-related with medical proof.[1]
